text: The surgeon initiates lateral to medial mobilization of the sigmoid and descending colon, progressing the cephalad dissection towards the splenic flexure.

The surgeon ligates the inferior mesenteric artery using a vessel sealer, extending the ligation to the level of the rectum. The mobilization of the colon is visually confirmed before proceeding with the transection of the rectum.
The surgeon uses a grasper to secure the proximal colon through the twelve millimeter trocar site. A Proline suture is applied to mark the rectum, preparing for future colostomy reversal.
